memory.summary: Leisure is tempting, but I need to crush this term project first. I'll skip the relaxation and tackle the chore immediately to clear my head.
That trivial chore barely moved the needle. I need to stop wasting time on instant wins and immediately pivot to the high-value term project.
I wasted another hour on trivial tasks; the term project is barely moving and Thursday stress is inevitable. I need to stop procrastinating immediately or face a massive crunch later.
Two hours of grinding pushed my progress to 42%, but I'm running on fumes with a two-hour sleep debt. The deadline is looming, so I need to keep pushing despite the fatigue.
Finally broke through the 50% mark on the term project, but my energy is critically low and sleep debt is piling up. I need to push harder now before the deadline hits.
Hit 65% progress in two hours, but my energy is critically low. I need to push harder now before the deadline hits.
I crushed the term project to 71%, but I completely blew past the instant-win chore deadline. My energy is at zero, yet that wasted easy point stings more than the fatigue.
I pushed through to 77% in just 1.5 hours despite hitting zero energy, proving the deadline pressure forces results even when I'm drained.
I dragged myself to 82% in one last sprint, but my energy is flatlined. The project is nearly done, yet I still feel the sting of that wasted chore point from earlier.
That 0.5-hour grind barely moved the needle on a trivial chore while my energy is dead; I wasted time chasing an instant win instead of resting.
Another 8% in two hours despite zero energy proves I can force results when the clock ticks. The project is almost done, but I'm running on fumes again.
Squeezed out the final 8% despite zero energy, proving I can force results when the clock ticks. The project is done, but my body is completely flatlined.

desires: Immediately secure a high-value 'instant win' on the trivial chore to validate my effort despite being flatlined, rather than risking the perception of leaving things unfinished.
Force my body to push past zero energy one last time to close the remaining 2% on the term project before the deadline looms, proving I can extract results even when drained.
Ignore the physical cost and social need for rest (sleep debt) to maintain the narrative of relentless output, prioritizing the immediate task completion over long-term recovery.

goals.description: Sprint the final 2% on the Term Project to hit exactly 100% completion immediately.
goals.rationale: I am at 98%; stopping now is unacceptable. My impulsive nature demands an immediate 'instant win' to close the loop, and my achievement axis (0.5) dictates that a partial finish is a failure. I will push past my physical fatigue to lock in the A.
